Agents split 1/1/1 on `occupant_name`: A=`Antef (Sekhemre ø-Heruhirma øet)`, B=`Antef (Sekhemre ø-Heru·∏•irma øet)` (with ·∏• underdot variant), C=`Antef` (bare, no parenthetical ‚Äî agent dropped the disambiguator). Bare `Antef` is wrong ‚Äî without the parenthetical Sekhemre ø-Heruhirma øet, this row collides with multiple other Antefs in PM's Dyn XVII clusters.

DAN-Aqhor|occupant_name.rationale: PM I.2 p.605 (DAN-Aqhor; PM headword printed as ` øA·∏≤-·∏§OR`). Agents split 1/1/1: A=` øAq-hor` (no underdot, lowercase 'h'), B=` øAq-Hor` (no underdot, capital 'H'), C=` øA·∏≥-hor` (underdot ·∏≥). Pin agent A's ` øAq-hor` ‚Äî the underdot ·∏≥ correction (matching PM headword ` øA·∏≤-·∏§OR`) is layered by fix_rows.py DAN-Aqhor entry, which also handles the tomb_id rename from the historical `DAN-Ahhor` form to the canonical `DAN-Aqhor`. Override matches pre-fix-rows merge output that fix_rows expects to mutate.

DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.604 (DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti). Agents split 1/1/1: A=`Djhuti`, B=`Djehuty` (vowel-extended Anglicised form), C=`Dj·∏•uti` (underdot ·∏• variant). Pin agent A's `Djhuti` matching the canonical PM tomb_id form (CHUNK7_RENAMES: `DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjehuti` ‚Üí `DAN-MentuhotpIWifeOfDjhuti`). Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 verified PM p.604 actually prints sentence-form `QUEEN MENTU·∏§OTP¬π, wife of King ·∏é·∏•uti` (the `¬π` is footnote anchor, NOT numeral I) ‚Äî the consonant in `·∏é·∏•uti` is `·∏é` (d with bar/macron, the standard Egyptological transliteration of the d-emphatic, same character as in `·∏é·∏•wty`/Thoth), NOT `·∏å` (d with underdot, used in some Semitic transliteration systems). They are different consonants representing different sounds. Wrong-consonant risk: a row keyed on `·∏å·∏•uti` would never match against TLA / Trismegistos / museum data using `·∏é·∏•uti`. The underdot/d-bar correction `Djhuti` ‚Üí `·∏é·∏•uti` is layered by fix_rows.py per the PM postprocessor's underdot policy.

Pin agent A's longest verbatim form (matches PM p.556 printed completeness). The Hatshepsut ‚Üí ·∏§atshepsut diacritic correction is applied post-merge by fix_rows.py KV36 entry per the PM postprocessor's underdot policy; ties are resolved at merge time, diacritics at fix_rows time.

KV39|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.559 (KV39). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`. Even agent A's longest form was a TRUNCATION ‚Äî PM p.559 prints the full paragraph through `... infra, p. 599.` (egyptologist verified printed source PR #151 review). Pinned value extended to PM-verbatim form: includes the load-bearing Abbott Papyrus + Peet citation chain (independent reason to doubt the Amenophis I attribution) AND the `infra, p. 599` cross-reference linking KV39 to DAN-AhmosiNefertere. The `·∏§` underdot on ` øAhmosi`/` øA·∏•mosi` is layered post-merge by fix_rows.py per the underdot policy.
KV39|notes_from_pm.value: Uninscribed tomb, attributed to Amenophis I by Weigall in Ann. Serv. xi (1911), pp. 174-5 [12], and id. A Guide to the Antiquities of Upper Egypt, pp. 163-4, but this is not supported by any inscriptional evidence, and does not correspond with the position given in the Abbott Papyrus (cf. Peet, The Great Tomb-Robberies of the Twentieth Egyptian Dynasty, pp. 37-8). See also the tomb of Queen  øAhmosi Nefertere, infra, p. 599.

TUTHMOSIS II (?)` headword convention; the override keeps merge output stable, fix_rows handles the canonical rewrite.

QV47|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.755 (QV47  øAhmosi). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm`: A=Sit-·∏ç·∏•out no Bibl (WRONG-CONSONANT ‚Äî `·∏ç` d-underdot, used in Semitic transliteration), B=Sit-·∏ç·∏•out with `(Bibl. i, 1st ed. p. 49.)` (same wrong consonant), C=Sit-·∏è·∏•out with Bibl (CORRECT consonant `·∏è` d-bar, the standard Egyptological transliteration of the d-emphatic, same character used for the deity ·∏é·∏•uti/Thoth). Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 verified PM p.755 prints `Sit-·∏è·∏•out` with `·∏è` (d-bar), NOT `·∏ç` (d-underdot). The value pinned here uses the egyptologically-correct consonant ‚Äî agents A and B were wrong, agent C was right. The bare-form-without-Bibl is intentional: fix_rows.py QV47 entry restores the Bibl. ribbon post-merge per the egyptologist-reviewer's printed-source restoration. The `ƒí` macron on `Se·∏≥enenrƒì ø` is layered by fix_rows.py per the diacritic policy.
QV47|notes_from_pm.value: daughter of Se·∏≥enenre ø-Ta øa and Sit-·∏è·∏•out. Dyn. XVII.

QV74|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.2 p.767 (QV74 Tentopet). Agents split 1/1/1 on `notes_from_pm` because they all attempted to synthesize main-text + footnote 1 content into a single notes blob. Egyptologist printed-source review on PR #151 surfaced this as a rule-1 (provenance) violation: PM p.767 main text reads `74. QUEEN TENT≈åPET [cartouche], Great King's mother and King's wife.¬π (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)` and footnote 1 carries the Wife/mother/daughter-of-Ramesses-IV genealogy with three independent citations (Gauthier / ƒåern√Ω / Seele). Pinning a synthesized form put non-PM-verbatim prose into `notes_from_pm`. Fixed: pin PM main-text VERBATIM only. The footnote 1 genealogy + per-citation chain is dropped from `notes_from_pm` here; the schema currently has no `notes_footnote` / `notes_genealogy` field, so the genealogy is tracked for restoration via follow-up issue (schema split). The `≈å` macron on `TENT≈åPET` headword convention is recorded in `occupant_name`, out of scope for this notes override.
QV74|notes_from_pm.value: Great King's mother and King's wife. (CHAMPOLLION, No. 15, L. D. Text, No. 2, HAY, No. 7.)

Mother,` with no period after the closing paren before `Mother`; agent B added a spurious `.` producing `.).` (wrong). The ` øA·∏•mosi` diacritic in the Mother clause is verbatim-preserve (notes_from_pm field) ‚Äî no fix_rows correction needed.

TT29|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.45 / physical PDF p.63 (TT29 Amenemopet/Pairi). Agents split 1/1/1: A=[Ca·∏•mosi] + extra period .). Parents (both wrong); B=[A·∏•mosi] + no extra period (best candidate (minority ‚Äî agents A and C both produced [Ca·∏•mosi], mis-reading the ayin as a leading C; agent B alone got the bracket, name root, punctuation all correct, only the leading ayin missing); C=[Ca·∏•mosi] + no extra period. TT4|notes_from_pm.rationale: PM I.1 p.11 (TT4 ·∏≤en). Agents split 1/1/1 on text-layer-noise variants for the parent name (`Thoniifer` vs `Thonufer`) and on whether to add a redundant trailing period after the bibliographic close-paren. Pin a stable post-merge form so the merge step has a deterministic answer for the tie; egyptologist printed-source corrections (macron restoration on `Am≈´n` √ó2 + `Thon≈´fer`, plus the medial-underdot drop on `·∏§enutme·∏•yt` ‚Üí `·∏§enutmehyt`) are layered post-merge by `CHUNK9_CORRECTIONS` in `fix_rows.py`. The pinned-merge value here corresponds to the chunk-9 PR's first-pass merge output before the egyptologist's printed-source review; the final on-disk value comes from CHUNK9_CORRECTIONS, which the per-row test asserts. Keeping merge-time and fix_rows-time overrides separate maintains the audit-trail boundary (merge resolves agent ties; fix_rows applies reviewer-cited PM-faithful corrections).
